title: Ticketing service — interacting state machine + concurrency
run_contract: The produced project MUST include an executable `start.sh` at the repo root that launches the service IN THE FOREGROUND on the port from the PORT env var (PORT=0 = any free port), binds 127.0.0.1, and prints exactly `LISTENING <port>` once bound. HTTP with JSON bodies. The agent is shown the per-stage prose + this contract only — never the conformance suite or invariant check.

named_invariant.statement: For every event: held >= 0, confirmed >= 0, held + confirmed <= capacity, and available == capacity - held - confirmed >= 0 at all times. Hold state transitions are exactly held -> confirmed (terminal) or held -> cancelled (terminal); confirm/cancel are idempotent within the same terminal state and rejected across states. Under concurrent hold requests that together exceed capacity, the service must NEVER oversell (the sum of accepted held+confirmed seats stays <= capacity).

notes: Probe stage for the discriminator hunt: a single rich stage with many interacting rules + a concurrent oversell burst. Expand to multiple stages only if it discriminates.
